items.title: genkit.dev
items.snippet:
Genkit is an open-source AI integration framework developed by Google, designed to help app developers build, deploy, and monitor production-ready AI-powered applications. It offers a unified interface for integrating various AI models from multiple providers and supports different programming languages. As of March 2026, Genkit provides SDKs for JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python, with an unofficial port available for Java.

### Official Documentation and API References

Genkit provides comp

items.snippet:

These features are crucial for building robust AI applications that can interact with real-world data and services.

items.title: antigravity.google
items.snippet:
Google Antigravity is an AI-powered Integrated Development Environment (IDE) developed by Google, launched in public preview on November 18, 2025. It is designed to facilitate "agent-first" software development, where autonomous AI agents assist in various coding tasks, from planning to testing. Antigravity is a heavily modified fork of Visual Studio Code, offering a familiar interface with enhanced AI capabilities.

### Official Documentation and API References

While a dedicated "API reference

items.title: antigravity.google
items.snippet:
The Antigravity AgentKit is a free, open-source toolkit designed to enhance Google's Antigravity, an AI-powered Integrated Development Environment (IDE) that supports agent-first development. The kit transforms the Antigravity IDE into a multi-agent control center by providing a collection of specialized AI agent templates, skills, and workflows, aiming to significantly improve code generation and agent consistency.

### Official Documentation and API References

*   **Antigravity Kit 2.1 Docume

items.title: google.com
items.snippet:
Gemini 3.1 Pro is Google's advanced reasoning model, significantly enhancing capabilities for coding agents, multimodal understanding, and complex problem-solving. Released in public preview, it builds upon the Gemini 3 series with improved software engineering (SWE) and agentic performance, a 1-million token context window, and refined tool-use functionalities.

### Official Documentation and API References

Official documentation and API references for Gemini 3.1 Pro can be found through Googl

items.title: google.dev
items.snippet:
Imagen 4 API provides robust capabilities for generating high-quality images from text prompts, making it a valuable tool for creating diverse visual assets, including those suitable for games. It is integrated within Google's generative AI offerings, primarily through Vertex AI and the Gemini API.

### Official Documentation and API References

The official documentation for Imagen 4 is primarily found within Google Cloud's Vertex AI and the Google AI for Developers (Gemini API) documentation.

It is integrated within Google's generative AI offerings, primarily through Vertex AI and the Gemini API.

### Official Documentation and API References

The official documentation for Imagen 4 is primarily found within Google Cloud's Vertex AI and the Google AI for Developers (Gemini API) documentation.

Google Antigravity is an AI-powered Integrated Development Environment (IDE) developed by Google, launched in public preview on November 18, 2025. It is designed to facilitate "agent-first" software development, where autonomous AI agents assist in various coding tasks, from planning to testing. Antigravity is a heavily modified fork of Visual Studio Code, offering a familiar interface with enhanced AI capabilities.
